demographics.living_situation: lives at home with both parents; complex home care set-up with specialised feeding equipment and medications

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: He has had four seizures this afternoon where his left arm twitches continuously, and his skin has turned dark yellow today. (spoken by the baby's father, who is holding him on the video call)
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The seizures keep coming back this afternoon and his usual seizure medicine isn't stopping them, and he's gone a much deeper yellow and is really hard to wake — the parents are frightened and don't know what to do.

symptom_narrative.onset: the twitching episodes started about 3 hours ago this afternoon
symptom_narrative.duration: roughly 3 hours; four separate episodes so far
symptom_narrative.character: his left arm and the side of his face twitch over and over for a minute or two, then stop, and in between he is floppy and very hard to wake
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the parents say this is the worst he has ever been and they are terrified
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: the twitching is mainly his left arm and face; the yellow colour is all over his skin and the whites of his eyes
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: the episodes keep coming back in a cluster this afternoon and he seems more drained after each one
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing they have tried has helped; his regular seizure medicine this morning has not stopped it
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: it seems to be getting worse on its own over the afternoon
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: he won't feed at all today, he isn't sucking, and he seems more yellow than his usual baseline
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: he has had some seizures before that were usually controlled by his regular medicine, but never a run like this
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: he is completely dependent; today he is barely responding to touch or voices

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: a suspected genetic condition affecting how his body breaks down certain fats — the parents were told it is likely a rare inherited condition and they are still waiting on the gene test to confirm it
past_medical_history.when_diagnosed: suspected since shortly after birth (4 weeks ago); genetic confirmation still pending
past_medical_history.how_managed: specialist metabolic and complex-care team follow-up, special feeds, and regular seizure medicine at home

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: very floppy and weak since birth (low muscle tone)
an unusual head and face shape the doctors noticed at birth (high forehead, soft spot larger than usual)
yellow skin (jaundice) that has been there in the background and feeding difficulties since birth

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: his regular seizure medicine (phenobarbital) — given this morning as usual

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: newborn cared for at home by both parents with a complex-care set-up; parents are vigilant but overwhelmed and very distressed today

paediatric_context.developmental_concerns: profound low muscle tone and feeding difficulty since birth; under a specialist metabolic/complex-care team; genetic testing pending

objective_data_offered.value: deep yellow discolouration of the skin all over, and the whites of the eyes look yellow
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: colour seen over a video call in home lighting; the depth of jaundice cannot be measured remotely

objective_data_offered.value: repeated twitching of the left arm and left side of the face lasting a minute or two at a time
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: seizure activity glimpsed on video; type, duration and frequency cannot be reliably characterised without in-person assessment

objective_data_offered.value: he is very floppy and limp, barely moving, and hard to wake or get a response from between the twitching episodes
he has not fed at all today and isn't sucking
his weight at home was about 3.2 kg today; he was 3.6 kg when he was born
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: home scale reading reported by parents; not a clinically calibrated measurement
